data.acceptance:
# Acceptance criteria

Each criterion is a verifiable check. Put a tick next to each only when
the corresponding test or grep returns the expected result. If a check
regresses later, this knot is the place to re-open.

## AC-1 — OpenCode has a canonical extractor

- `normalizeOpenCodeModel(rawModel)` exists in
  `src/lib/agent-identity.ts` alongside the other per-provider
  normalisers.
- It parses the canonical `<router>/<vendor>/<model-with-version>` shape
  (e.g. `openrouter/moonshotai/kimi-k2.6` →
  `{ model: "openrouter/moonshotai/kimi-k2.6",
     flavor: "openrouter", version: "2.6" }`).
- It NEVER pulls a version from the OpenCode binary version, an env
  var, or any runtime hint.
- `normalizeAgentIdentity` calls `normalizeOpenCodeModel` for the
  OpenCode branch, replacing the current passthrough at
  `agent-identity.ts:243-250`.
- A new test file `src/lib/__tests__/agent-identity-opencode.test.ts`
  covers all OpenCode model id shapes Foolery has seen
  (`openrouter/moonshotai/kimi-k2.6`,
  `openrouter/anthropic/claude-sonnet-4-5`,
  `openrouter/z-ai/glm-5.1`,
  `openrouter/mistral/devstral-2512`,
  bare `kimi`, etc.).

**Verify:** `bun run test src/lib/__tests__/agent-identity-opencode.test.ts`
passes; the screenshot row "OpenCode openrouter/moonshotai/kimi-k2.6 4.7"
becomes "OpenCode openrouter/moonshotai/kimi-k2.6 2.6" (or empty version
if Kimi K2.6 doesn't expose a numeric version we want to surface — to be
agreed by the planner).

## AC-2 — `toActiveAgentInfo` is removed or gutted

`src/app/beats/to-active-agent-info.ts` no longer calls
`normalizeAgentIdentity`. Either:

- (a) it is deleted entirely and `useAgentInfoMap` reads canonical fields
  directly off the stamped capsule / step-history entry, or
- (b) it is reduced to a pure label formatter that takes already-canonical
  `{ agentName, model, version }` and produces an `AgentInfo` for
  display, with no parsing.

**Verify:** `grep -rn "normalizeAgentIdentity\|normalize.*Model" src/app/`
returns zero hits.

## AC-3 — Terminal store stops holding raw agent fields

`src/stores/terminal-store.ts` no longer has `agentName`, `agentModel`,
`agentVersion`, `agentCommand` as separate fields. Replaced by:

- a `knotsLeaseId: string` (and/or `claimedKnotId: string`) on
  `ActiveTerminal`, and
- a hook that, given a lease id, returns the lease's `agent_info` (or
  the bound knot's most recent step's stamped fields) on demand.

The display path in `useAgentInfoMap` reads the lease/knot data, not
terminal-store agent fields.

**Verify:** `grep -n "agentName\|agentModel\|agentVersion\|agentCommand"
src/stores/terminal-store.ts` returns zero hits in the field
declarations (mentions inside resolver helpers that pull from the lease
are fine).

## AC-4 — Multi-key fallbacks are removed from readers

`src/components/beat-table-metadata.tsx` resolves agent fields by reading
ONE canonical key per concept:

- agent name → `agent_name` on step records, `agentname` on capsules /
  notes (each artifact type uses ONE key, the one Knots stamps)
- model → `agent_model` / `model`
- version → `agent_version` / `version`

The fallback chains `["agentname", "agentName", "agent_name"]` etc.
disappear. If a record is missing a field, the renderer either shows
nothing or fails loudly (per
`feedback_no_fallbacks_hard_fail_loudly.md`) — it does not invent.

**Verify:** `grep -n '"agentName"\|"agentname"\|"agent_name"'
src/components/beat-table-metadata.tsx` shows at most one constant
string per artifact type.

## AC-5 — Display formatting is one function

Every label-rendering site uses `formatAgentDisplayLabel` and/or
`parseAgentDisplayParts` from `src/lib/agent-identity.ts`. The custom
`buildAgentLabel` in `src/components/agent-history-utils.tsx:391` is
either deleted or delegates to one of those.

**Verify:** `grep -rn "function buildAgentLabel\|const buildAgentLabel"
src/` returns zero hits OR the function is a one-line passthrough.

## AC-6 — Self-audit greps return zero unsanctioned hits

The four greps in `docs/knots-agent-identity-contract.md` § "Self-Audit"
all return zero hits (other than the canonical extractor file itself
and tests). Specifically:

- per-provider normalisers are imported only by `agent-identity.ts` and
  by tests;
- `toActiveAgentInfo` / `normalizeAgentIdentity` are not called outside
  `agent-identity.ts` and `src/app/beats/`;
- no `--agent-name` / `--agent-model` / `--agent-version` is passed to
  any `kno` command other than `lease create`;
- no `process.env.*MODEL*` / `process.env.ANTHROPIC*` / etc. is read
  for agent identity.

**Verify:** Run all four greps from the contract doc. Each must come
back empty.

## AC-7 — `createLease` test covers every provider

`src/lib/__tests__/knots-canonical-metadata.test.ts` has a
`describe("createLease canonical fields", ...)` block with one `it()`
per provider — Claude, Codex, Gemini, Copilot, OpenCode — that:

- starts from the registered-config shape Foolery would actually have
  in `settings.toml`,
- runs it through `toCanonicalLeaseIdentity` →
  `toExecutionAgentInfo` → `createLease`,
- asserts the exact `kno lease create` argv that comes out, and
- asserts nothing identity-related is missing or duplicated.

**Verify:** Provider count in the describe matches the
`AgentProviderId` enum in `src/lib/agent-identity.ts:3` minus
`"unknown"`.

## AC-8 — Two-repo doc parity

The Foolery contract doc and the Knots `leases.md` agree:

- both reference each other,
- both list the same artifact types Knots stamps,
- both state "lease is the only place identity is declared" and
  "clients must not re-derive,"
- both have a flow diagram (Foolery: extraction; Knots: stamping).

**Verify:** Reading both top to bottom in one sitting reveals no
contradictions and no unique-to-one-side rules.

## AC-9 — A `kno lease show <id>` round-trip test exists

A new test (location TBD by planner — likely
`src/lib/__tests__/knots-lease-roundtrip.test.ts`) creates a lease for
each provider, fetches it back via `kno lease show --json`, and
asserts the canonical fields round-trip without loss. This catches
silent shape drift between Foolery's `CanonicalLeaseIdentity` and
Knots' `AgentInfo` struct.

**Verify:** `bun run test knots-lease-roundtrip` passes.

## AC-10 — The visible bug is fixed

Open the beat table in the running app. The OpenCode row no longer
shows a phantom "4.7" version. The Claude rows no longer show
"Claude Opus 4.7  Claude Opus 4.7  4.7" with the family doubled — they
show the family once, then the version, with no redundancy. Take a
screenshot and attach it as a note on this knot for posterity.

**Verify:** Side-by-side screenshots: pre-fix from this knot's filing
date (2026-04-30, see screenshot in the originating message) vs.
post-fix.
